content: Hey, I've been trying to find more balance between work and my hobbies lately. Working as a UX researcher at Meta can get pretty intense.
That sounds like a demanding job! What kind of hobbies help you unwind after long days of user research and design work?
I've been getting back into DJing - I used to spin at college parties and I miss it. Also picked up archery during the pandemic and I'm surprisingly good at it.
That's an interesting combination! How do you balance something as high-energy as DJing with the focus required for archery?
They actually complement each other well. DJing lets me express creativity and connect with people, while archery is my meditation - I go to this range near Papago Park a few times a week.
Papago Park sounds beautiful. Have you lived in Phoenix long? I thought most tech workers were still concentrated in the Bay Area.
I moved here from San Francisco about 18 months ago. The cost of living was killing me and I realized I could do my job remotely. Phoenix has been great - I've got a nice setup in my home office now.
That's a big change! What do you miss most about the Bay Area, if anything?
Honestly, just the food scene. I've been trying to perfect my coffee brewing at home since there aren't as many good cafes around here. I've got this fancy pour-over setup now.
Coffee brewing is such a satisfying hobby! Do you experiment with different beans and methods?

Absolutely. I'm part of this coffee subscription service that sends beans from different roasters. Ethiopian Yirgacheffe is my current favorite. On weekends I sometimes host board game nights too - just taught everyone Catan last month.
Sounds like you've built a nice community there! What's your go-to board game when you have people over?

Codenames, hands down. It's simple enough for newcomers but still strategic. I've been thinking about learning origami too - saw some amazing modular designs online and it seems like a good complement to my other hobbies.
Origami would be a great addition! The precision would probably help with your archery too. Have you played tennis recently? I remember you mentioning that earlier.

Yeah, I play doubles with some friends at the community courts on Saturdays. It's a good workout and way more social than archery. Between all these hobbies and work, I'm pretty busy but it's a good kind of busy.
